page: Amazon Web Services d3.4xlarge is a dense storage instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 8 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A defining characteristic of this server is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 23,760 GB of local HDD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing tasks, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the weak category. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks. Memory bandwidth for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks remains in the average tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also perform at average levels.

The primary tradeoff of the d3.4xlarge is its reliance on high-capacity local HDD storage rather than solid-state drives, which limits random I/O performance but offers exceptional qualitative cost efficiency for high-volume sequential data storage. The hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring massive local storage capacity alongside moderate compute and memory resources. It is highly suitable for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems like HDFS, MapReduce jobs, and massive log processing pipelines where sequential read and write throughput is prioritized over low-latency random access.
description: Amazon Web Services d3.4xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for data-intensive workloads requiring massive local storage capacity. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The server is equipped with 23,760 GB of local HDD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results show weak single-core CPU performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for sequential storage density, making it a strong fit for distributed file systems, data warehousing, MapReduce, and large-scale log processing where massive sequential storage is required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3.4xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 23,760 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for sequential data workloads and distributed file systems.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3.4xlarge features 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 23,760 GB of local HDD storage for dense sequential data workloads.
tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for large-scale sequential data processing and distributed filesystems.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory
Massive 23,760 GB of local HDD storage for dense data requirements
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core performance contrasted with weaker single-core execution
Optimized for distributed file systems, MapReduce, and sequential log processing
